name: Richard M. Jones
abstract: Richard M. Jones, born Richard Marigny Jones (sometimes written Richard Mariney Jones), (June 13, 1892 – December 8, 1945) was an American jazz pianist, composer, band leader, and record producer.

about_quotes.speaker: Natty Dominique
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with him in Chicago. I worked with him in New Orleans. ... My last recording was with Richard Miney Jones on the Decca Company.

about_quotes.speaker: Willie Hightower
about_quotes.evidence: Did you make some records with Richard M. Jones too? ... No, I remember the band because I used all of my band. Just Richard on piano.

about_quotes.speaker: Ernest 'Punch' Miller
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, I made records with him. ... I worked with him in Chicago.

about_quotes.speaker: Warren "Baby" Dodds
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Richard M. Jones only in the context of the song 'New Orleans hop, scop blues' and describes him as 'a fellow, a very good friend of mine' referring to George W. Thomas, not Richard M. Jones. There is no evidence of a personal or professional relationship between Baby Dodds

about_quotes.speaker: "Wooden Joe" Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Who else was in the band when Oliver was there at Abedee's? ... Was it Richard M Jones? [INTERVIEWEE] Oh yeah. Miney, Miney's name was, we used to call him Miney. Miney Jones. Joseph Jones.

about_quotes.speaker: Billy McBride and Mary McBride ("Mack and Mack")
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Richard M. Jones taking the interviewer to the Dreamland, but the interviewee does not mention any personal interaction or relationship with Richard M. Jones.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy McBride and Mary McBride ("Mack and Mack") Interview, 1959-07-01
